sentences.base_language: I went for a run this morning.
sentences.grammarNote: 跑步了 — the 了 at the sentence end marks a completed action or new situation: 'I went for a run (and I did it).' It confirms the activity happened, often with a sense of reporting.
Duration pattern: verb + 了 + time duration. 跑了三十分钟 = 'ran for thirty minutes'. If an object follows, add another 了: 我跑了三十分钟的步 (I ran for thirty minutes of jogging).
Body-part as topic: 我腿很酸 (my legs are sore) — the body part (腿) is the topic and comes right after the subject 我. This topic-comment structure is very common in Chinese.
之后 (zhīhòu) = 'after'. More formal than 后 alone. 之前 (zhīqián) = 'before'. Pattern: action + 之后/之前 = after/before the action.

sentences.base_language: I take a shower after exercising.
sentences.grammarNote: 运动完 = 'finish exercising'. 完 (wán) as a resultative complement after a verb indicates completion. 运动完 → 做完 (finish doing), 吃完 (finish eating), 看完 (finish watching).

能 (néng) expresses capability or possibility: 'can / is able to'. Compare with 可以 (kěyǐ, permission/possibility) and 会 (huì, learned ability or prediction). 能 focuses on physical or situational ability.

sentences.base_language: I try to exercise at least three times a week.
sentences.target_language: 我每周尽量锻炼至少三次。
sentences.romanized: Wǒ měi zhōu jǐnliàng duànliàn zhìshǎo sān cì.
sentences.literal: I every week try-to exercise at-least three times.
sentences.grammarNote: 尽量 (jǐnliàng) = 'as much as possible / try to'. 至少 (zhìshǎo) = 'at least'. 每周 = 'every week' (每 + time unit). 次 (cì) = measure word for occurrences: 三次 = three times.

sentences.romanized: Wǒ jīntiān shēntǐ gǎnjué hěn hǎo.
sentences.literal: I today body feels very good.
